models.code:
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customer'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    balance = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    credit_limit = Column(Decimal, default=0)
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customer.id'))
    amount_total = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    notes = Column(String)
    date_shipped = Column(Date)
models.description: A class to represent orders placed by customers, including notes.
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'item'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('order.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('product.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal, default=0)
    amount = Column(Decimal, default=0)
models.description: A class to represent individual items in an order.
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal, default=0)
models.description: A class to represent products available for sale.
models.name: Product

rules.name: CustomerCreditConstraint
rules.description: Ensure that a customer's balance is under their credit limit.
rules.code: Rule.constraint(validate=Customer, as_condition=lambda row: row.balance <= row.credit_limit, error_msg="Customer balance ({row.balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.credit_limit})")

rules.name: CustomerBalanceCalculation
rules.description: Calculate customer's balance as sum of order total amounts where not yet shipped.
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.balance, as_sum_of=Order.amount_total, where=lambda row: row.date_shipped is None)

rules.name: OrderTotalCalculation
rules.description: Calculate order amount total as sum of item amounts.

rules.name: ItemAmountCalculation
rules.description: Calculate item amount as quantity multiplied by unit price.

rules.name: ItemUnitPriceCopy
rules.description: Copy the product's unit price into the item's unit price.

rules.name: SendOrderToKafka
rules.description: Send the order to Kafka topic 'order_shipping' if date shipped is not None.
rules.code: Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka, if_condition=lambda row: row.date_shipped is not None, with_args={"topic": "order_shipping"})

test_data:
# Creating test data rows for customers, orders, items, and products
customer1 = Customer(name="Alice", balance=Decimal('3000'), credit_limit=Decimal('5000'))
customer2 = Customer(name="Bob", balance=Decimal('2000'), credit_limit=Decimal('4000'))
customer3 = Customer(name="Charlie", balance=Decimal('1500'), credit_limit=Decimal('3500'))
customer4 = Customer(name="David", balance=Decimal('1200'), credit_limit=Decimal('3000'))

product1 = Product(name="Product A", unit_price=Decimal('50'))
product2 = Product(name="Product B", unit_price=Decimal('30'))
product3 = Product(name="Product C", unit_price=Decimal('20'))
product4 = Product(name="Product D", unit_price=Decimal('10'))

order1 = Order(customer_id=customer1.id, amount_total=Decimal('150'), notes="Urgent delivery", date_shipped=None)
order2 = Order(customer_id=customer2.id, amount_total=Decimal('75'), notes="Standard delivery", date_shipped=None)
order3 = Order(customer_id=customer3.id, amount_total=Decimal('90'), notes="Express delivery", date_shipped=date(2023, 8, 20))
order4 = Order(customer_id=customer4.id, amount_total=Decimal('110'), notes="Gift package", date_shipped=date(2023, 8, 21))

item1 = Item(order_id=order1.id, product_id=product1.id, quantity=3, unit_price=Decimal('50'), amount=Decimal('150'))
item2 = Item(order_id=order2.id, product_id=product2.id, quantity=5, unit_price=Decimal('15'), amount=Decimal('75'))
item3 = Item(order_id=order3.id, product_id=product3.id, quantity=3, unit_price=Decimal('20'), amount=Decimal('60'))
item4 = Item(order_id=order4.id, product_id=product4.id, quantity=11, unit_price=Decimal('10'), amount=Decimal('110'))
